title: Best 11 Herbal Creams for Instant Inflamed Gums Relief

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Flavonoids are good for inflamed gums because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling, ease pain, and promote healing in the affected areas.

Alkylamides in Glycyrrrhhiza glabra are good for inflamed gums because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and ease pain in the gums, making them a natural remedy for treating gum inflammation.
Quercetin is good for inflamed gums because it has strong anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and ease pain in the gums.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea cream can be helpful for inflamed gums because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties. This means it can reduce swelling and ease pain in the gums. The cream has compounds like alkylamides and phenolic acids that help fight off infections and reduce inflammation. When applied to the gums, it may help stop the spread of infection and promote healing. The cream may also help reduce redness and discomfort in the gums, making it easier to eat and speak. Some people believe that Echinacea purpurea cream can help prevent gum disease and other oral health issues. The cream is often used in combination with other oral care products, like toothpaste and mouthwash, to keep the mouth healthy. Regular use of Echinacea purpurea cream may lead to healthier gums and a reduced risk of gum disease.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a herbal cream that helps with inflamed gums using Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice root, start by preparing the herb. You can dry the licorice root and grind it into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle. Next, mix 2-3 tablespoons of licorice root powder with 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of coconut oil in a small saucepan over low heat. Stir the mixture until the beeswax melts and the licorice root powder is fully incorporated. Remove the saucepan from the heat and let it cool slightly. Then, add 1-2 tablespoons of a carrier oil such as olive or jojoba oil and 1 teaspoon of vitamin E oil to the mixture and stir well. Pour the mixture into a small tin or jar and let it cool completely before using it. When you apply the cream to your gums, gently massage it in to help soothe and reduce inflammation. Be sure to do a patch test before using the cream to ensure you don't have any sensitivity to the ingredients.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal creams for inflamed gums

Aloe-emodin has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in inflamed gums by blocking the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and chemicals.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams are good for inflamed gums because they have anti-inflammatory properties. This means they can help reduce swelling and pain. The cream has a compound called aloe-emodin, which is known to fight bacteria and fungi that can cause infections. When you apply the cream to your gums, it helps to soothe and calm them, reducing the redness and inflammation. The cream also has antibacterial properties, which helps to prevent infections from spreading. Aloe barbadensis creams are also rich in vitamins A, C, and E, which can help to promote healing and protect the gums from further damage. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, aloe barbadensis creams can help to alleviate the symptoms of inflamed gums and promote a healthy mouth.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a soothing Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal cream for inflamed gums, start by infusing the dried roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra in a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil. Combine 2 tablespoons of dried roots with 8 ounces of carrier oil in a clean glass jar, and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every few days. After the infusion period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container, discarding the solids. Next, mix 2 tablespoons of beeswax with 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a small saucepan over low heat, stirring until the beeswax is fully melted. Combine the infused oil with 2 tablespoons of aloe vera gel and 2 drops of tea tree oil, then slowly pour the mixture into the saucepan with the melted beeswax. Continue stirring until the mixture thickens and turns into a smooth, creamy consistency. Pour the cream into small containers, and let it cool before use. To apply, gently massage a small amount onto the inflamed gums, 2-3 times a day, for relief and soothing comfort.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal creams for inflamed gums

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia, also known as tea tree oil, is a natural ingredient often used in creams to help soothe inflamed gums. This is because the oil has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and fight off the bacteria that cause gum infections. When applied to the gums, the cream can help calm pain and discomfort, making it easier to eat and speak. The anti-inflammatory properties of Melaleuca alternifolia can also help reduce redness and swelling, making the gums look healthier and more normal. Additionally, tea tree oil has been shown to be effective against a range of oral health issues, including gum disease and mouth ulcers. When used in a cream, it can be easily applied to the affected area, providing quick and effective relief from gum inflammation.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a soothing Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal cream for inflamed gums, you will need dried licorice root, coconut oil, beeswax, and a double boiler. First, mix 1/4 cup of dried licorice root with 2 cups of water in a saucepan, and then boil the mixture for 10-15 minutes to create a licorice root infusion. Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl, and discard the solids. Next, melt 1/2 cup of coconut oil and 2 tablespoons of beeswax in a double boiler over low heat. Once the beeswax has fully melted, slowly add the licorice root infusion to the melted oil and wax mixture, stirring constantly to avoid lumps. Continue stirring until the mixture has cooled and thickened into a creamy consistency, then pour it into a clean container and let it cool completely before use. This soothing cream can be applied directly to inflamed gums using a cotton swab or a clean finger, providing relief from discomfort and promoting healing.
remedies.image_alt: Melaleuca alternifolia herbal creams for inflamed gums

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a herbal cream that helps with inflamed gums using Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice root, you'll need a few simple ingredients. First, dry and grind 2-3 tablespoons of licorice root into a fine powder. Next, mix the powder with 2 tablespoons of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of coconut oil, and 2 tablespoons of shea butter in a small saucepan over low heat. Stir the mixture until the beeswax and shea butter have melted, then remove it from the heat. Add 2 tablespoons of aloe vera gel and 2 drops of peppermint essential oil to the mixture and stir well. Once set, use a small amount of the cream to gently massage onto inflamed gums, 2-3 times a day, to help soothe and reduce inflammation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus cream is a natural remedy that can help soothe inflamed gums. The active ingredient in this cream, eucalyptol, has strong anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and ease pain. This makes it easier to brush and floss your teeth without causing more irritation. Eucalyptol also has antibacterial properties, which help fight off the bacteria that cause gum infections and inflammation. When applied directly to the gums, the cream creates a protective barrier that prevents further irritation and promotes healing. Regular use of eucalyptus globulus cream can reduce inflammation, ease pain, and promote healthy gum tissue growth. The cream is also gentle on gums and does not cause any allergic reactions, making it a safe and effective treatment option for people with sensitive gums.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal cream that helps with inflamed gums, start by preparing the herbal extract. First, dry Glycyrrhiza glabra roots in a low-temperature oven (around 150°F) for a few hours to remove moisture. Then, grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder. Next, mix one tablespoon of the powder with one cup of boiling water to create an herbal infusion. Let it steep for 10-15 minutes, then strain the liquid using a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to get a clear extract. Combine the extract with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil in a 1:10 ratio, and add a thickening agent like beeswax or candelilla wax if desired. Finally, heat the mixture gently to melt the wax, then pour it into a container and let it cool to form a solid cream. This cream can be applied directly to the inflamed gums to help reduce inflammation and soothe the affected area.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis cream has anti-inflammatory properties that can help with inflamed gums. The cream contains compounds like carnosic acid and rosmarinic acid, which can reduce swelling and pain. When applied to the gums, these compounds can help calm the inflammation and promote healing. remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal cream that helps with inflamed gums, start by harvesting or purchasing dried roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice root. Then, dry the roots further by spreading them on a paper bag in a low-temperature oven at 150-200°F (65-90°C) for 2-3 hours. Next, grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle. Combine the powder with a carrier oil such as coconut oil or olive oil in a ratio of 1:5, and heat the mixture in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water until the powder dissolves. Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool. Then, add a thickening agent like beeswax or candelilla wax, and mix until it's fully incorporated. Finally, pour the mixture into small containers, let it cool and solidify, and apply it to inflamed gums 2-3 times a day. It's essential to note that you should do a patch test before using the cream, and consult with a healthcare professional or a qualified herbalist for guidance on the dosage and preparation of the cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, is a natural remedy that has been used for centuries to help with various health issues. When it comes to inflamed gums, ginger cream can be very beneficial. The anti-inflammatory properties in ginger help to reduce swelling and pain in the gums. This is because ginger contains compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. When applied topically as a cream, these compounds can directly target the inflamed area and help to calm it down. This can lead to a reduction in gum pain, swelling, and even bleeding. Additionally, ginger has antimicrobial properties, which can help to fight off the bacteria that can cause gum infections. By using a ginger cream on inflamed gums, you may be able to reduce the severity of your symptoms and promote healthy gum healing. The soothing and calming effects of ginger can also help to reduce stress and anxiety, which can further contribute to gum health issues. Regular use of ginger cream may also help to prevent future gum infections and promote overall gum health.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal creams for inflamed gums, start by gathering the necessary ingredients. You will need dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root, coconut oil, beeswax, vitamin E oil, and a double boiler. Begin by steeping the dried root in hot water to make a tea, then strain the liquid and let it cool. In a separate bowl, mix together the coconut oil and beeswax, and place them in the double boiler. Heat the mixture until the beeswax is fully melted, then slowly add the cooled root tea to the mixture, stirring constantly. Continue to heat the mixture until it reaches a smooth and creamy consistency. Remove the double boiler from the heat and stir in the vitamin E oil. Pour the mixture into a clean container and let it cool before using. To apply, gently massage a small amount of the cream onto the inflamed gums, ideally after brushing and flossing. The anti-inflammatory properties of Glycyrrhiza glabra may help to soothe and calm the inflamed areas, promoting healthy gums and overall oral well-being.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita cream, also known as peppermint cream, is a natural remedy that can help soothe inflamed gums. The menthol in peppermint has a cooling effect that can reduce swelling and ease pain. When applied to the gums, the cream can numb the area, making it less sensitive and easier to handle. Additionally, peppermint's antibacterial properties can help fight off any underlying infections that may be causing the inflammation. The cream can also help to reduce bad breath and gum odor by killing bacteria in the mouth. When used regularly, peppermint cream can help to calm inflamed gums and promote healing.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a herbal cream for inflamed gums using Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice root, start by preparing the herb. First, dry the licorice root in a low-temperature oven at 150-200°F (65-90°C) for 2-3 hours or air-dry it for several days. Next, grind the dried licorice root into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle. Then, mix 2-3 tablespoons of the licorice root powder with 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of coconut oil in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water. Stir the mixture until the beeswax and coconut oil are fully melted and well combined with the licorice root powder. Remove the bowl from the heat and let the mixture cool slightly before whipping it with a hand mixer or electric beater until it thickens and becomes a smooth, creamy texture. Finally, pour the cream into a glass jar and store it in the refrigerator. To use, apply a small amount of the cream to the inflamed gums and gently massage it in. Repeat as needed for relief from gum inflammation.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a soothing herbal cream from Glycyrrhiza glabra that helps with inflamed gums, start by infusing dried licorice root in a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil. Combine 1 cup of dried licorice root with 2 cups of carrier oil in a clean glass jar, and let it sit in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every few days to help the infusion process. After the infusion is complete, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Next, mix a small amount of the infused oil with a base cream, such as aloe vera or shea butter cream, until you achieve your desired consistency. remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa creams, also known as turmeric creams, are a natural remedy for inflamed gums. The active ingredient curcumin in turmeric has powerful anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. When applied topically to the gums, it can reduce swelling and pain by blocking the production of substances that cause inflammation. It also has antibacterial properties, which help to fight off the bacteria that can cause gum infections and make them worse. Additionally, curcumin can help to reduce the amount of plaque and tartar on the teeth, making it easier to maintain good oral hygiene. remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal cream to help with inflamed gums, you need a few simple ingredients and some basic equipment. First, dry the roots of the licorice plant (Glycyrrhiza glabra) and then grind them into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle. Next, mix 2-3 teaspoons of this powder with 1 cup of coconut oil or olive oil in a small saucepan over low heat. Stir well until the powder dissolves completely and the mixture reaches a temperature of around 120°F to 140°F (49°C to 60°C). Remove the saucepan from the heat and let it cool slightly before straining the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container. Add a few drops of vitamin E oil and a tablespoon of beeswax to the mixture and stir well. Pour the mixture into a small tin or jar and let it cool completely before using it as a topical cream to soothe and calm inflamed gums.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal cream for inflamed gums, you need some basic ingredients and a few simple steps. First, you'll need to dry the roots of the Glycyrrhiza glabra plant, also known as licorice root, by spreading them on a paper or a tray and letting them air dry for a few days. intro_desc: Herbal creams can be a great help when dealing with inflamed gums. One reason is that they contain soothing herbs like Echinacea purpurea, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and pain. Echinacea also has antibacterial properties, which can help fight off the underlying infection causing the inflammation. Aloe barbadensis, another key ingredient, is known for its calming and anti-inflammatory effects. It can help reduce redness and ease discomfort.  Inflamed gums can lead to more serious issues, such as gum disease or even tooth loss if left untreated. This can affect not only your dental health but also your overall confidence and well-being. When you have healthy gums, you can eat and smile without pain or worry. You can also prevent the spread of infection to other parts of your body.  Using herbal creams on inflamed gums can be a safe and natural way to treat the issue. Simply apply the cream to the affected area, usually 2-3 times a day, and let it work its magic. The key is to catch the problem early and be consistent with your treatment. By doing so, you can prevent further complications and enjoy a healthy, pain-free smile.
supplementary_best: A combination of 'Aloe barbadensis', 'Melaleuca alternifolia', and 'Curcuma longa' herbal creams can help remedy inflamed gums. 'Aloe barbadensis' has anti-inflammatory properties, which can soothe and calm swollen gums. 'Melaleuca alternifolia' contains compounds like eucalyptol and cineole, which have antimicrobial properties, helping to reduce bacterial infections that cause gum inflammation. 'Curcuma longa', or turmeric, contains curcumin, which is an anti-inflammatory compound that can reduce swelling and pain in the gums. This combination works well because it addresses both the inflammation and the underlying bacterial infection that causes it. The anti-inflammatory properties of 'Aloe barbadensis' and 'Curcuma longa' complement the antimicrobial properties of 'Melaleuca alternifolia', making it a balanced and effective remedy for inflamed gums.
supplementary_avoid: If you have inflamed gums, it's best to avoid herbal creams containing the following herbs:  Valerian root (Valeriana officinalis) - can cause allergic reactions and interact with certain medications. Licorice root (Glycyrrhiza uralensis) is also problematic because of its potential to increase blood pressure and interact with medications. Ginkgo biloba was already excluded, but note that it's often combined with other herbs, so check the ingredients. Capsicum (Capsicum annuum), or cayenne pepper, can irritate gums further. Clove (Syzygium aromaticum) and wintergreen (Gaultheria procumbens) contain salicylates, which are similar to aspirin and can cause bleeding in inflamed gums.
supplementary_other_remedies: Saltwater rinse can help with inflamed gums. When you swish saltwater in your mouth, it helps reduce swelling and kills bacteria that can cause the inflammation. This is because saltwater has antibacterial properties and can help draw out the extra fluid from the gums, reducing swelling.  Aloe vera juice can also be beneficial for inflamed gums. Aloe vera has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm the gums, reducing pain and discomfort. When you swish aloe vera juice in your mouth, it can help reduce inflammation and promote healing.  Probiotics can help maintain good oral health and prevent gum inflammation. Probiotics are beneficial bacteria that can help keep your mouth clean and free from disease-causing bacteria. When you take probiotics, they can help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the gums.  Oil pulling with coconut oil or sesame oil can also help with inflamed gums. Oil pulling involves swishing oil in your mouth for a few minutes to help remove bacteria and other debris from your teeth and gums. This can help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the gums.  Baking soda can help reduce inflammation in the gums by helping to neutralize the acid that can cause inflammation. When you mix baking soda with water to create a paste, you can apply it to your gums to help reduce inflammation and promote healing.  These remedies can help alleviate inflamed gums by reducing inflammation, promoting healing, and preventing disease-causing bacteria from growing in the mouth.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with inflamed gums, also known as gingivitis, may experience other health issues related to their gums and mouth. They are more likely to develop bad breath (halitosis) because of the bacteria in their mouth that causes the gum inflammation. This is because the bacteria in their mouth can release strong-smelling gases.  Some people with inflamed gums may also experience mouth ulcers (aphthous ulcers), which are small sores that can be painful and make eating and drinking difficult. This is because the inflammation in their gums can spread to other parts of their mouth.  Inflammated gums can also lead to a condition called periodontitis, which is a more serious infection of the gums that can cause the teeth to become loose and eventually fall out. This is because the bacteria in their mouth can infect the bone that supports their teeth.  Some people with inflamed gums may also experience headaches and facial pain, because the inflammation in their gums can cause pain to spread to other parts of their head and face.  Medicinal herbs like aloe vera, chamomile, and turmeric can help heal inflamed gums and related health issues. Aloe vera has anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe and calm the gums, while chamomile can help reduce swelling and pain. Turmeric contains a compound called curcumin, which has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can help fight the infection in the gums and reduce the risk of developing periodontitis.  Astragalus and licorice root can also help reduce inflammation and kill the bacteria that causes gum inflammation. Echinacea can help boost the immune system, which can help fight off the infection in the gums.  However, it is essential to note that while medicinal herbs can help alleviate symptoms, they should not replace regular dental check-ups and proper oral hygiene.
